dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3065 IS: Let Americans Buy with Explicit Labeling Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mrs. Hyde-Smith (for herself and Mr. Tuberville) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry

form.official-title: To amend the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 to strengthen country of origin and method of production labeling for fish.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Let Americans Buy with Explicit Labeling Act or the LABEL Act.

section.header: Country of origin and method of production labeling for fish
section.text: Section 282(c) of the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 (7 U.S.C. 1638a(c)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: In the case of a covered commodity that is farm-raised fish or wild fish, the information required by subsection (a) shall be provided to consumers by a means described in paragraph (1) in a conspicuous location, so as to render the information likely to be read and understood by a consumer under normal conditions of purchase, in a font size that is not smaller than the font size describing the farm-raised fish or wild fish on the package, display, holding unit, or bin.

section.text: This Act and the amendments made by this Act shall take effect on the date that is 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act.
